message.content:
Write two memory files and update MEMORY.md for the user Nicholas Clement. The memory directory is at /Users/nicholas/.claude/projects/-Users-nicholas/memory/. Do NOT create the directory — just write files directly into it.

---

FILE 1: Write to /Users/nicholas/.claude/projects/-Users-nicholas/memory/user_profile.md

Content:
```
---
name: User Profile — Nicholas Clement
description: Nicholas's professional background, role, and goals for resume work
type: user
---

Senior Software Development Engineer (Sr. SDE) at Amazon Web Services with 7+ years of experience. Currently on the AWS Data Center Systems team (April 2024 - Present). Previously spent ~2 years at AWS Supply Chain (Aug 2022 - Apr 2024) building pure cloud microservices.

Key strengths: cloud-native distributed systems, REST API design, DynamoDB, technical leadership (led teams of up to 13 engineers), AI-augmented development, application security (AppSec Guardian across two orgs).

Primary languages: Python, Kotlin, Java, TypeScript/JavaScript.
AWS services: Lambda, ECS/Fargate, API Gateway, DynamoDB, SageMaker, S3, CloudWatch, CDK, Step Functions, CloudFormation, Amplify, SSM.

Holds TS/SCI with FSP clearance (Jan 2023 - current).
Located in Denver, CO. Interested in remote positions.
CU Boulder BS Computer Science, class of 2018.
```

---

FILE 2: Write to /Users/nicholas/.claude/projects/-Users-nicholas/memory/project_resume_2026.md

Content:
```
---
name: Resume Project 2026
description: Nicholas is tailoring his resume for remote cloud SWE roles at multiple companies
type: project
---

Nicholas is building a set of tailored resumes to apply to remote software engineering positions.

**Why:** He wants to move from a datacenter-focused role to broader cloud SWE opportunities at remote companies.

**How to apply:** When helping with resume work, the base/generic version is at /Users/nicholas/Desktop/Resume-2026/nicholas-clement-base.md. Company-specific tailored versions should be derived from this file. The original Oracle-tailored version (datacenter/PE focus) is at /Users/nicholas/Desktop/Resume-2026/march-2026-general-resume.md. An older 2025 JSON version (Reactive Resume format) is at /Users/nicholas/Desktop/Resume-2026/2025/nicholasclement2025.json.

Key changes made in the base version vs the Oracle-tailored version:
- Headline reframed from datacenter-focused to cloud-native/distributed systems
- Internal AWS codenames translated to plain descriptions (e.g., MegloOperatorService → "On-Demand Device Polling API")
- SCADA/BMS/Modbus/BACnet jargon removed or minimized
- Skills section added (was missing from markdown version)
- Kotlin added back (was missing despite being primary language for Supply Chain work)
- Personal projects section restored (Happy Hour Finder, Map-based Art Generator)
- Intern entries dropped (too old for Sr. SDE resume)
```

---

FILE 3: Read /Users/nicholas/.claude/projects/-Users-nicholas/memory/MEMORY.md first, then append the two new entries to it. If MEMORY.md doesn't exist, create it with just the index entries (no frontmatter).

The entries to add:
- `[User Profile — Nicholas Clement](./user_profile.md)` — Sr. SDE at AWS, cloud architect, Denver CO, seeking remote SWE roles
- `[Resume Project 2026](./project_resume_2026.md)` — Building tailored resumes for remote cloud SWE positions; base file at Desktop/Resume-2026/nicholas-clement-base.md
